summary: This source provides a brief biographical overview of the ancient Greek philosopher Socrates. It notes that he authored no texts himself, leaving his philosophy to be reconstructed through the contradictory accounts of his students like Plato and Xenophon, primarily through the question-and-answer format of Socratic dialogues.

frameworks.name: Socratic Dialogue
frameworks.when_to_apply: When examining a subject or philosophical concept with interlocutors.
steps: Engage with interlocutors to examine a subject.
Use a style of continuous question and answer to explore the topic.
frameworks.source_quote: examine a subject in the style of question and answer
